entry.title: Cranberries: The Secret To Boosting Athletic Performance? #research #athlete #varsity #supplements

media:group.media:title: Cranberries: The Secret To Boosting Athletic Performance? #research #athlete #varsity #supplements
media:group.media:description:
Antoine Denis, PhD student in performance research, and Dr. Andreas Bergdahl, Associate Professor of Cardiovascular & Performance Physiology, give us a closer look at the HKAP Training Centre with researchers working in the Bergdahl Cardiovascular and Performance Physiology Lab!
From the latest episode in our Closer Look series, where 4th Space explores the spaces and projects across Concordia that you might not otherwise get a chance to see!
In case you missed it, you can explore the lab by clicking the link above 🔗

media:group.media:title: Unveiling Equity: Traces of Home
media:group.media:description:
As part of the Unveiling Equity series, in partnership with CSLP, CPN-PREV, and the Indigenous Healing Knowledges Concordia Research Chair, this event invites a critical reflection on loss, memory, and resistance amid the ongoing demolition of villages in South Lebanon. Beyond physical destruction, the conversation centers the erasure of lived histories, homes that held childhoods, photographs, books and everyday life.
Framed as a “genocide of memory,” it asks: what does it mean to lose not only land, but the intimate archives of belonging? How do communities grieve when the material anchors of memory are erased?
An oral history dimension grounds the discussion across three generations: a daughter remembering a father who passed before witnessing the demolition of the home he built in his village; a son carrying the memory of a his Palestinian father; and a young man holding onto his grandparents’ call to return to Bint Jbeil as home.
The conversation also opens into an open mic, inviting participants to share their own stories of loss and grief, creating a collective space of witnessing. Through decolonial perspectives, speakers explore storytelling and remembrance as forms of resistance, and asks how dignity, identity, and connection to place can be reclaimed in the face of erasure.
Speakers
Diana Moukalled A co-founder and managing editor of Daraj Media, an independent media platform founded in 2017 in Beirut focusing on covering corruption and underreported stories.
Guillaume Jabbour French Canadian Palestinian, Guillaume Jabbour centers his practice around sound, research-creation and community engagement.
Jad Orphée Chami Jad Orphée Chami is an artist-researcher and composer from Beirut, based in Tiohtià:ke/Montreal.
Zeina Ismail Allouche She is the Research Coordinator at the Centre for the Study of Learning and Performance (CSLP) and the Associate Director of CPN-PREV.

entry.title: From Mississippi to Montreal: Telling His Story #activism #civilrights #memoir #montreal

media:group.media:title: From Mississippi to Montreal: Telling His Story #activism #civilrights #memoir #montreal
media:group.media:description:
Fred Anderson, civil rights activist, author, and community leader, discusses the writing of his book during a conversation on his journey from the Civil Rights Movement in Mississippi, to fleeing the US under a different name, to social advocacy in Montreal.
In case you missed it, you can watch the full conversation with Fred Anderson and Kelann Currie-Williams by clicking the link above 🔗

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Worker Health and Pollution II

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Worker Health and Pollution II
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Lauren Laframboise, join for another session from the DéPOT 2026 Conference, where the panelists explore the Worker Health and Pollution II . The conference looks to explore different aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.
Rebekah Chatellier, “‘You just got on with it.’: Acceptable Risks and Health and Safety in the Rural Cotton Mills of Stanley (Scotland) and Remerton (Georgia, USA)”
Guillaume Tremblay-Boily, “De la Canadian Steel Foundries au parc-nature Mercier-Hochelaga-Maisonneuve : un terrain industriel au cœur des luttes environnementales »
Laurence Hamel-Roy, « La surveillance et le contrôle des risques d’exposition à l’amiante chez les travailleur·euse·s de la construction à l’épreuve de l’emploi : quelle(s) sécurité(s)? »
Amandine Tabutaud, « Quand la désindustrialisation rend malade : souffrances physiques et morales des ouvrières de Seine-Saint-Denis et de Haute-Vienne (années 1980 au très contemporain) »
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions are the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining II

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining II
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Philippine D’Halleine, join for another session from the DéPOT 2026 Conference, where the panelists explore the Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining II. The conference looks to explore different aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.
Eagle Glassheim, “The Future Is in the Past: Envisioning Post-Mining Futures on Minnesota’s Iron Range”
Romain Castellesi, “‘Des mineurs contre l’environnement ? Écologie politique, reconversion territoriale et identité ouvrière dans un bassin minier rural du sud-ouest de la France (Carmaux, 1983-2003) »
Inès Delpuech, “Nuisances visibles, nuisances indicibles : les mémoires minières des dégradations environnementales dans la Tunisie du XXe siècle »
Melina Buns, « Remediating a Uranium Mine, Removing Industrial Traces, Reshaping the Landscape”
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions are the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Dirty Old Town: Deinsdustrial Art (Expo Tour/Présentation des artistes)

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Dirty Old Town: Deinsdustrial Art (Expo Tour/Présentation des artistes)
media:group.media:description:
Join us as we explore and meet the artists behind the Dirty Old Town Exhibition at 4th Space!
Artists and researchers from around the world come together to investigate the myriad of impacts that deindustrialization can have on individuals, communities and the environment.
Artists:
Russell Gendron Karine Bellerive Remy Chhem & Paul Tom David Lewis G. Scott Macleod Eliot Perrin Vanessa Randall
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions will be the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Industrial and Energy Transitions
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Mircea Raianu, join for another session from the DéPOT 2026 Conference, where the panelists explore the Industrial and Energy Transitions. The conference looks to explore different aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.
01:31 Mircea Raianu 02:18 Séverine Misset and Anaël Marrec, “Un estuaire en transition : conflits et alliances face aux transformations énergétiques de l’estuaire de la Loire (1970-2020) » 26:28 Petra Dolata and William Gillies, “The Power Politics of Deindustrialization: Managing Pit Closure in Canada and Germany during the Era of Energy Crisis, 1968-1980” 47:16 Q&A
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions are the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Mathis Gronau, join for another session from the DéPOT 2026 Conference, where the panelists explore the Deindustrialization and the Environment in Post-Socialist Europe. The conference looks to explore different aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.
01:18 Intro Mathis Gronau 02:28 Ondřej Ševeček and Vítězslav Sommer, “Shifting Burdens: The Life Cycle of Footwear, Labor, and the Environment from Socialist Central Planning to Global Supply Chains” 19:27 Nina Vodopivec, “Deindustrialization, Environmentalism, and Transition: The Case Study of Post-Socialist Slovenia” 38:03 Juliette Ronsin and Loman-Pierre Charrier, “L’automobile, une industrie «polluante » condamnée comme la mine ? Perspectives comparées autour de la fonderie de Sept-Fons et de l’usine de Sochaux du groupe Stellantis » 56:35 Q&A
What can the study of deindustrialization add to our understanding of environmental history? And conversely – what can the study of environmental history contribute to deindustrialization studies? Answering these questions are the focus of the 2026 Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) annual conference, whose theme is “Deindustrialization and the Environment.”

entry.title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining I

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: Environmental Pasts and Futures in Mining I
media:group.media:description:
Moderated by Pascal Raggi, join us for one of the first sessions from the DéPOT 2026 Conference! This panel explores the environmental pasts and futures in mining.
00:00 02:15 Opening Remarks 4th Space 03:41 Montaine Pichot, “En quête d'un compromis fluide : compromis social sur l'eau et fermeture des mines de fer en Lorraine (1991-2001)” 19:41 Sarah MacDonell, “Sodded Coal Mine: the promise of environmental rehabilitation” 37:57 Théo Georget, “Les luttes ouvrières en France et l’arme de la pollution industrielle » 57:13 Q&A Period

media:group.media:title: DéPOT 2026: A Collective Book Launch
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the collective book event celebrating recent publications produced by the Deindustrialization and the Politics of Our Time (DePOT) research project!
00:00 Intro 4th Space 01:21 Opening Remarks by Steven High 20:16 Steven High and Stefan Berger, eds. _The Politics of Industrial Closure: Transnational Patterns and Comparisons_ 28:10 Pascal Raggi et Fanny Gallot, dir. _Ouvriers et ouvrières dans la désindustrialisation._ 31:55 Indranil Chakraborty and Steven High, eds. _Deindustrialization and Economic Restructuring in Post-Reform India._ 36:15 Cory Haala, _When Democrats Won the Heartland: Progressive Populism in the Age of Reagan, 1978-1992_ 40:50 John Sandlos and Arn Keeling, _The Price of Gold: Mining, Pollution and Resistance in Yellowknife._ (McGill Queen's University Press). 52:39 Tom Fraser, Invested in Crisis: _Public Sector Pensions Against the Future_
This is part of the (Un)just Transitions? Deindustrialization and the Environment conference that looks to explore these and other aspects of the intersection between deindustrialization and the environment.

entry.title: Agents in the Field: Agentic AI for Climate Science and Ecologywith Genevieve Patterson

media:group.media:title: Agents in the Field: Agentic AI for Climate Science and Ecologywith Genevieve Patterson
media:group.media:description:
Join us for the first part of the Leadership in Environmental and Digital innovation for Sustainability (LEADS) Wrap-up Event.
Agentic AI systems can now plan, write, and execute scientific workflows with limited human intervention. This talk shows how familiar tasks for ecologists, biologists, and climate scientists can be reframed as modular, agent-driven pipelines. I demonstrate recently popular approaches to ecological case studies from my own work, including macaque behavior tracking and green crab molting detection. The focus is on practical capabilities, limitations, and how to keep these systems scientifically reliable.
AI systems can now plan, write, and execute scientific workflows with limited human intervention.
Part of LEADS Wraps Up!
LEADS is Leadership in Environmental and Digital innovation for Sustainability (LEADS) is an NSERC-CREATE graduate program on the theme of science leadership for global sustainability in the digital age.

entry.title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026: Projection et discussion avec Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo

media:group.media:title: Plurilinguisme en Éducation 2026: Projection et discussion avec Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo
media:group.media:description:
Rejoignez-nous pour le dernier événement de Pluri-Ed-J 2 : Plurilinguisme en action – perspectives, ressources et pratiques!
Projection et discussion du documentaire en anglais "No Vernacular! - A Journey Through Ghana’s Multilingual Classrooms" par Mama Adobea Nii-OwooNo ("Vernacular! - Un voyage au cœur des classes plurilingues au Ghana).
PLURI-ED-J-2 mettra de l’avant des modèles issus de la recherche, des ressources didactiques et pédagogiques, des ateliers ainsi que des pistes concrètes pour mettre le plurilinguisme en action dans différents contextes éducatifs.

entry.title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media

media:group.media:title: Unboxing with Concordia University Press: Illuminating Media
media:group.media:description:
Join us with Saelan Twerdy as we unbox Illuminating Media: Transmitting the Renaissance in England, 1400–1550.
Bridging medieval studies, media studies, design history, and communications, Illuminating Media highlights the surprising endurance of these visual strategies in contemporary culture, from the design of passports to the layout of currency. Written in crisp, engaging prose, the book offers a new way of seeing both medieval art and modern design, making a powerful case for the value of interdisciplinary scholarship.
This book is now available in print and open access at press.library.concordia.ca.
